channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Authorities ramp up efforts to protect sea turtles as deaths on India's shores continue to mount
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Dead sea turtles keep washing ashore on India's east coast and environmentalists and authorities are trying frantically to stop the number of 600 dead from getting even higher.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A bid to block Trump's cancellation of birthright citizenship is in federal court
item.description: A federal judge in Seattle is set to hear the first arguments Thursday in a multi-state lawsuit seeking to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents' immigration status. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A blooming plant that reeks of gym socks and rotting garbage has thousands lining up for a whiff
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — The rare unfurling of an endangered plant that emits the smell of decaying flesh drew hundreds of devoted fans to a greenhouse in Sydney on Thursday where they joined three-hour lines to experience a momentous bloom -– and a fragrance evoking gym socks and rotting garbage.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and RICK RYCROFT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How to protect your home and belongings in case of a fire or emergency
item.description: Ken Maccarone joins "Morning in America" to learn vital strategies to protect yourself and your loved ones during a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Ken Maccarone, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger to appear in court as defense challenges prosecution methods
item.description: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger will return to court for the first time in more than two months to argue for excluding nearly all evidence collected by police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Gulf of Whatnow? Mapmakers grapple with Trump's geographic renaming plans
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's DEI regulations across government to 'backfire': Lawyer
item.description: President Trump slashed DEI programs, giving agencies 60 days to cut all positions and to proclaim there are only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly half of remote workers say they'd quit before returning to office full-time
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Nearly half of workers who currently work remotely at least some of the time would likely leave their jobs if ordered to return to the office full-time. At least, that's the key finding in a new survey from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, ABC 4

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont worked at the Pentagon during 9/11, family says
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border was a military veteran who worked security duty at the Pentagon during the Sept. 11 attacks, his family said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Murdoch's UK tabloids apologize to Prince Harry and admit intruding on the late Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry claimed a “monumental” victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology for intruding in his life for years, and agreed to pay substantial damages to settle his privacy invasion lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Historic snowfall in Florida inspires ‘Blizzard of '25’ charity shirt to help the homeless
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (BLOOM) — Snow in Florida? Believe it. The Sunshine State is embracing its frosty moment with the launch of the “I Survived the Blizzard of '25” t-shirt, a playful nod to the historic snowfall that left Floridians shivering—and snapping selfies.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, KRON4

item.title: Texas man who tried to scam ex-US Rep. George Santos sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Texas man was sentenced to a year and a half in prison Wednesday over his effort to dupe George Santos out of nearly $1 million by offering to destroy evidence in the disgraced congressman’s corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks' policy change flushes out a debate over public restroom access
item.description: Starbucks’ decision to restrict its restrooms to paying customers has flushed out a wider problem: a patchwork of restroom policies that has left Americans confused and divided over who gets to use the loo and when. Rules about restroom access in restaurants vary by state, city and county.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats grill Project 2025 co-author over ‘illegal’ Trump funding move
item.description: Democrats on Wednesday grilled Office of Management and Budget (OMB) nominee Russell Vought over recent executive orders made by President Trump they say “illegally” target funding authorized by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas man says he ‘accidentally’ shot girlfriend in head before driving with body for almost 2 hours: police
item.description: A man who drove around with his dead girlfriend in his passenger seat for hours asked his friend, who called 911 to report him, to help him get rid of her body in the desert after he had "accidentally" shot her in the back of the head, police documents said.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has moved to end affirmative action in federal contracting and directed that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing risk of sending kids to school
item.description: Fears intensified for some when the Trump administration announced Tuesday it would allow federal immigration agencies to make arrests at schools, churches and hospitals, ending a decades-old policy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire chief gunned down while helping stranded driver
item.description: CHAMBERS COUNTY, Ala. (WRBL) – A Coweta County battalion fire chief was killed Sunday while stopping to help a stranded driver on County Road 267 in Chambers County, Alabama, according to the Chambers County Sheriff’s Office. The incident occurred shortly before 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5
